continue: Two textbook easy runs back-to-back — Saturday's long run (avg HR 128) and today's 8-miler (avg HR 129). THIS is the aerobic base a sub-3 build is made of, and it pushed your easy share up to 33%.
Fully recovered and green: ACWR back to 1.0 (optimal), readiness “rested & ready,” HRV balanced, 8.7 h sleep last night. You absorbed last week's intervals + long run cleanly.
Volume baseline is rising — your 4-week average jumped to ~17.6 mi/wk (28 in the last 7). That's the right trajectory toward the 40–50 mi/wk the goal needs.
change: Polarized split is improving — easy miles up to 33% and the gray zone down to 47% (from 50%), hard ~20%. Keep going toward ~80% easy / ~20% hard. The occasional run that drifts hard (e.g. keeping pace with a fast colleague) is what pads the gray zone — fine once, just don't let it become the default.
Build the aerobic base — Garmin still flags “Aerobic Low Shortage.” Keep stacking easy Z2 volume; it's the #1 driver toward sub-3 and what lifts VO2max (52.4) over the coming months.

Weight wobbled to ~175.8 yesterday after a salty, carby weekend but already settled to 173.7 today — that's the water-weight whoosh, not fat. The trend is still gently down toward your low-160s; watch the weekly average, not the daily.
This is a travel/deload week (Germany Tue→Tue). Keep everything easy and skippable around jet lag — you're banking consistency, not chasing mileage. Resume the build when you're home.
Sleep's been strong (86 last night, 8.7 h). Protect it through travel — it's your best jet-lag and recovery tool, and what lets you absorb rising mileage without breaking down.
